Enhanced Perinatal Support Program
About Us
The
Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP)
provides a wide range of
						culturally competent services to Medi-Cal pregnant women, from conception through 1 year
						postpartum. In addition to standard obstetric services, women receive enhanced services in the
						areas of nutrition, psychosocial, and health education. The
Legislature enacted CPSP in
							1984
in response to the
OB Access Project
, which found that a
						comprehensive approach reduced both low birth weight rates and health care costs in women and
						infants. CPSP became a Medi-Cal benefit in 1987. Medi-Cal Managed Health Care Plans are required
						to use the most current standards of ACOG and CPSP for pregnant Medi-Cal eligible
						recipients.
Mission
Through effective and collaborative teamwork, CPSP supports public and private
								obstetrical providers to coordinate the delivery of
early, continuous and
									quality comprehensive perinatal services
to pregnant and postpartum people
								and their infants.
Vision
All pregnant and postpartum people in Los Angeles County
receive comprehensive perinatal health services through an integrated and seamless system designed to optimize maternal and child health outcomes.
Goals
To decrease
the incidence of low birth weight in infants
To improve
the outcome of every pregnancy
To give
every baby a healthy start in life
To lower
health care costs by preventing catastrophic and
										chronic illness in infants and children
To assist
providers with CPSP implementation and perinatal
										health education
To establish
continued connection between managed care plans
										and public and private obstetrical providers

Find a CPSP Provider
The
Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program (CPSP)
is a Medi-Cal benefit that
						provides a variety of culturally competent services to individuals from pregnancy through 1 year
						postpartum. CPSP is a program that enhances obstetric care through assessments and interventions
						that include health education, nutrition, emotional and social support.
To view a list of CPSP providers offering CPSP services in Los Angeles County
(excluding
							Long Beach and Pasadena)
, use the filter tool below.
If a provider on this
							list is identified as not providing CPSP currently
, please email the CPSP
						Administration office at
cpsp@ph.lacounty.gov
. Patients enrolled
						in a managed care health plan should contact their plan for referral to a contracting physician.

Disclaimer
This is not a list of all
OB
							providers in Los Angeles County
. It contains only those CPSP providers affiliated
						with the Los Angeles County Enhanced Perinatal Support Program
(excluding the cities of
							Pasadena and Long Beach)
who are currently offering CPSP services.
Note:
If your clinic is not listed or is not actively offering CPSP services,
						please contact us via email at
cpsp@ph.lacounty.gov
.
